pulse: The industrial sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 17.2x and a P/E of 28.9x, which is generally in line with market valuations though select companies show significant disparity. For instance, GE (ev_to_ebitda 34.2x, trailing pe 42.2x) and CAT (ev_to_ebitda 26.9x, trailing pe 38.9x) have reported robust revenue growth of 17.6% and 18.0% respectively, highlighting their operational efficiency amid the recent BofA outlook predicting a new industrial cycle with potential for 25%+ earnings upside. Looking ahead, investors should consider the implications of the upcoming CNH Industrial earnings call, which will address margin pressures and cost-cutting measures that could reshape profitability in a landscape influenced by China's industrial profit growth nearing a two-year high.
